name: Castillo de Miravet - Circular by the Ebro river & Meandro del Tamarigar
name_original: Castillo de Miravet - Circular por el rio Ebro & Meandro del Tamarigar
description: This easy 3.2-mile hike circles Miravet Castle, offering scenic views of the Ebro River and the charming village.
region_intro: Hiking around Miravet offers diverse terrain shaped by the Ebro River, which meanders through the landscape, creating riparian areas and islands. The region features hills crowned by the historic Templar Castle, providing elevated viewpoints over the river and the old town. Trails traverse riverbanks, olive groves, and lower mountain slopes, presenting a varied natural environment for exploration. Best hiking trails around Miravet The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Miravet - Circular by the Ebro river & Meandro del Tamarigar, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete, offering views of the Ebro River and passing through the Tamarigar nature reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Miravet Castle – View of Miravet Castle loop from Miravet, a difficult 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the Ebro River and the village from elevated positions around the castle. Local hikers also love the Miravet — Village and Riverside – Miravet Castle loop from Miravet, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through the historic old town and along the Ebro riverbanks, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Hiking around Miravet is defined by the Ebro River, the strategic Templar Castle, and the charming old town. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging climbs around the castle. The routes in Miravet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Miravet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Miravet?
A: Miravet offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the Ebro River, the historic Templar Castle, and the charming old town, providing a rich natural and cultural experience.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking in Miravet?
A: The trails around Miravet range in difficulty. You'll find a good selection of easy routes, with 14 trails classified as easy, 4 as moderate, and 3 as difficult. This variety ensures there's something for everyone, from leisurely riverside strolls to more challenging climbs around the castle.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while hiking in Miravet?
A: Many hiking routes in Miravet are intertwined with its rich history. The iconic &lt;strong&gt;Templar Castle of Miravet&lt;/strong&gt;, perched atop a hill, is a central feature, offering panoramic views of the Ebro River and the village. You can also explore the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Old Town (Cap de la Vila)&lt;/strong&gt; with its narrow, cobbled streets, which can be integrated into longer itineraries. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6931181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet — Village and Riverside – Miravet Castle loop from Miravet&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the historic old town and along the riverbanks.

Q: Are there any natural features or viewpoints to discover on Miravet's trails?
A: Absolutely. The Ebro River is a dominant natural feature, with trails like the GR99 following its banks, offering tranquil river beaches and views of islands and meanders. The &lt;strong&gt;Tamarigar nature reserve&lt;/strong&gt; is ideal for bird watching. For stunning vistas, the &lt;strong&gt;La Sanaqueta viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; within the old town provides an excellent perspective of the Ebro River's meander. Additionally, you can find natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4510439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Prior&lt;/a&gt; and various summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/692380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Santos Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3021692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; in the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Miravet?
A: Yes, Miravet offers several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951843598/castillo-de-miravet-circular-by-the-ebro-river-meandro-del-tamarigar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miravet - Circular by the Ebro river & Meandro del Tamarigar&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views of the Ebro River and passes through the Tamarigar nature reserve. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle – View of Miravet Castle loop from Miravet&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views from elevated positions around the castle.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Miravet?
A: The hiking experience in Miravet is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the captivating blend of historical landmarks, diverse natural landscapes, and the intimate connection with the Ebro River. The variety of trails, from riverside paths to routes around the Templar Castle, is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Miravet?
A: Miravet is a great destination for families, offering several easy routes suitable for all ages. Many trails follow the Ebro River, providing gentle walks with beautiful scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951843598/castillo-de-miravet-circular-by-the-ebro-river-meandro-del-tamarigar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miravet - Circular by the Ebro river & Meandro del Tamarigar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km route that takes about 1 hour 35 minutes, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Miravet?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail may vary, generally, the natural environment around Miravet is welcoming to dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in nature reserves like the Tamarigar, and to clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty for everyone. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Miravet?
A: Miravet offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, with pleasant weather and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers cooler, crisp air, ideal for longer treks.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through Miravet?
A: Yes, the long-distance GR99 (Camino del Ebro) route passes through Miravet. This trail follows the Ebro River, offering extensive riverside treks and connecting Miravet to a wider network of paths. It's an excellent option for those looking for multi-day adventures or longer sections of hiking.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Miravet's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Miravet is quite varied. You can expect paths along the tranquil Ebro River banks, trails that ascend hills to the Templar Castle, and routes that traverse olive groves and lower mountain slopes. The region features a rich contrast of landscapes, from lush riparian forests to more rugged, elevated areas, ensuring a diverse hiking experience.

Q: Are there options for parking near the hiking trailheads in Miravet?
A: Miravet is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, often close to popular starting points for trails. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas, especially near the Old Town or the access points to the Ebro River paths, to ensure convenient access to the trailheads.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Miravet's hiking trails?
A: Miravet is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services that connect it to larger towns in the Terres de l'Ebre region. Once in Miravet, many of the hiking trails, particularly those around the village, the castle, and along the Ebro River, are within walking distance from the village center, making them accessible without a private vehicle.
tips: Beautiful town located on the banks of the Ebre River and with a wildlife reserve in front, on the islands of the Ebre River.

Miravet Castle is located in a strategic point dominating the course of the Ebro river and the adjacent territory. An imposing fortress with spectacular views. The castle stands on a hill and is protected by a wall up to 25 m high, which has witnessed some of the most significant moments in this country.
https://patrimoni.gencat.cat/es/monumentos/monumentos/castillo-de-miravet
Old mill located at the foot of the Ebro river. In the past it must have been a small dam since the word "assut" means barrier in the Arabic language.

The old church of Miravet was built on an old mosque.
The effects of the projectiles from the Battle of the Ebro can still be seen on the façade.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular hike?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available within the charming village of Miravet. From there, you can easily access the starting points for the circular route along the Ebro river and towards the castle.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an easy hike, making it suitable for beginners and families. It features easily-accessible paths and is designed for various fitness levels. While some parts of Miravet village have cobbled streets, the trail along the river is generally natural terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally easy, with accessible paths. You'll experience a mix of natural riverside trails along the Ebro and potentially some cobbled streets within the medieval village of Miravet — Village and Riverside. There's an elevation gain of about 170 meters, but it's spread out, making it manageable.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a rich blend of history and nature. You'll enjoy captivating views of the Ebro River and its meanders, such as the Meandro del Tamarigar. A prominent feature is the impressive Miravet Castle, a 12th-century Templar fortress perched above the river. You'll also pass by the Mill and Arab Weir and the Church of the Nativity of the Virgin Mary, Miravet, all while experiencing the medieval charm of Miravet village.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas of the village and to protect local wildlife along the river. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the Ribera d'Ebre region with comfortable weather. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable if hiking during those months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific circular trail along the Ebro river and around Miravet. Access to the natural areas and paths is free. However, if you plan to enter Miravet Castle, there may be an admission fee.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is approximately 5.22 kilometers (about 3.24 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. This duration can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will find parts of the E39, El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Camí De Benifallet - Colada de la Mola Del Broi along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or photo opportunities on the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail offers numerous photographic opportunities. The views of Miravet Castle from various perspectives, the winding Ebro River, and the charming village itself are particularly stunning. The pier in Miravet is specifically noted as a great spot for photos of the river and the hanging houses.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail circles through and around Miravet village, you will find cafes and pubs where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike. The village offers a pleasant atmosphere to enjoy local food and drinks.

waypoints.name: Church of the Nativity of the Virgin Mary, Miravet
